nodes.name: Telegram Trigger
nodes.notes:
1. The front door.

Every reel starts when you send a message to the bot on Telegram.
This node listens for two kinds of updates:
  - message = a text like '/reel claude' or plain 'claude'
  - callback_query = you tapping an Approve/Reject button on a card.
One Telegram bot = one webhook URL, so both kinds of events land here
and get sorted downstream.

nodes.name: Parse Command + Authorize
nodes.notes:
3b. Read the message and decide what to do.

- Checks the sender's Telegram user id against the allowlist
  (anyone else is ignored silently).
- Understands: /reel, /help, /status, /cancel, /clear
- Tolerates typos on slash commands (e.g. /rel -> /reel).
- If the message is plain text like 'claude', treats the WHOLE
  text as a topic for /reel.
- Checks if we already made cards for the same topic in the last
  hour. If yes, asks you to confirm with /reel! <topic>.
- Detects brand keywords (claude -> anthropic, gemini -> deepmind,
  etc.) so later nodes fetch the official RSS first.
- Sends /help, /status, /cancel, /clear replies directly and
  returns [] (stops the flow), only /reel continues past here.

nodes.name: Fetch All RSS Feeds
nodes.notes:
5. Pull the latest news from 6 AI companies.

Six RSS feeds (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google AI, DeepMind, Meta AI,
Hugging Face) fetched in parallel. Each one is wrapped in its own
try/catch, so one dead feed doesn't kill the whole run.
Anthropic and Meta killed their official RSS feeds, so for those
two we fall back to Google News RSS searches that aggregate the
same stories from third-party sites.
If the topic mentions a specific brand, that brand's feed is
fetched first (priority ordering).

nodes.name: Normalize & Dedupe
nodes.notes:
6. Clean up the pile.

We have ~90 raw stories from the 6 feeds. This node:
  - drops anything older than 7 days
  - removes duplicates (same title across feeds)
  - strips HTML tags from descriptions
If nothing is left after cleanup, we throw a clear error.
The error workflow catches it and tells you on Telegram.

nodes.name: Build Filter Prompt
nodes.notes:
7. Write instructions for the cheap AI.

Builds the exact text we'll send to Claude Haiku (the fast,
cheap model). The instructions say:
  - here are ~60 stories, each numbered
  - here's the user's topic
  - pick the 3 most relevant stories, topic-first
  - if nothing matches, return []
  - if the user has a brand preference, weight the preferred
    source more heavily.

nodes.name: Claude Rank
nodes.notes:
8. The cheap AI picks the 3 best stories.

Sends the prompt to Claude Haiku 4.5 (fast model, ~$0.001 per
run). Haiku reads the stories and returns a JSON array like
[4, 12, 37], just three indices.
We use Haiku for this step specifically because it's 10x cheaper
than Sonnet and this is just a sorting task, not creative work.

nodes.name: Select Top 3
nodes.notes:
9. Grab the 3 winning stories.

Reads Claude Haiku's [4, 12, 37] response and maps those indices
back to the actual stories. If Claude returns [], we send a
friendly DM explaining no matches were found (with which feeds
are returning stories + which ones failed) and stop cleanly ,
no red error alert, just useful info.

nodes.name: Build Scene Plan Prompt
nodes.notes:
10b. Classify the topic's archetype, then build an archetype-specific prompt.

Instead of handing Claude a single recipe (which made every reel look
the same), we classify each candidate into one of 7 archetypes using
keyword rules:
  launch_announcement  (default, 'X launched Y')
  technical_deep_dive  (benchmarks, API, code)
  business_partnership (deals, acquisitions, enterprise)
  feature_capability   ('X now has Y')
  controversy_policy   (regulation, lawsuits, safety)
  industry_stats       (user counts, market share)
  story_impact         (human case studies)

Each archetype has its own scene kit (scenes to prefer) and avoid
list (scenes that feel wrong). Claude picks beats from the kit, so
two different archetypes produce visibly different reels even for
the same brand.

nodes.name: Claude Scene Plan
nodes.notes:
11. The smart AI writes the reel script.

Sends the prompt to Claude Sonnet 4.6 (larger, smarter, ~$0.03
per call). Sonnet returns a full JSON spec, title, beats with
scene types and props, brand, color palette.
Higher token budget (4000) than the ranking step because scene
plans are rich JSON documents.

nodes.name: Parse Scene Plans
nodes.notes:
12. Read what Sonnet wrote.

Sonnet sometimes wraps JSON in prose. We extract the first {...}
block, parse it, and validate the basic shape (must have beats,
target duration, etc.). If it's not valid JSON, we throw with
the first 200 characters of Sonnet's actual response so the
error message is debuggable.

nodes.name: Format Telegram Cards
nodes.notes:
13. Build 3 preview cards.

Takes the 3 scene plans and builds one Telegram message per
candidate. Each message shows:
  - 'Candidate A/B/C' label + source brand
  - reel title + first 5 beats
  - two inline buttons: Approve + Reject
The button callback_data encodes (approve/reject, job_id,
candidate_index) so WF2 knows exactly what to render on tap.

nodes.name: Send Cards to Telegram
nodes.notes:
14. Post the 3 cards to the user.

Three sendMessage calls to Telegram, one per card. We retry
up to 3 times on transient failures (e.g. rate limits).
At this point the user sees the three options in their chat.

nodes.name: Prep Job File
nodes.notes:
16. Package everything into one JSON blob.

Builds the full job object:
  - when it was created
  - who asked (chat_id, from_id)
  - the original topic string
  - the preferred source (for audit)
  - status: 'pending' (changes to 'cancelled' if /cancel fires)
  - all 3 candidates with their full scene plans
This JSON is written to disk in the next node so WF2 can read
it when the user approves a candidate.

nodes.name: Write Job File
nodes.notes:
17. Save the job to disk.

Writes /project/jobs/<jobId>.json, a shared folder mounted into
both the n8n container and the Windows host. This is the bridge
between WF1 (creating) and WF2 (rendering). Using a real file
instead of in-memory state also makes debugging much easier ,
you can cat the file to see exactly what WF2 will read.

nodes.name: Is Callback Query?
nodes.notes: Routes Telegram updates. TRUE (top) = user tapped an Approve/Reject button on an existing card -> hand off to Workflow 2 for render. FALSE (bottom) = user sent a fresh /reel command -> kick off research pipeline.

nodes.name: Call WF2 (Render)
nodes.notes:
3a. Hand a button tap to the render workflow.

Fires when you tap Approve or Reject on a candidate card. We call the render workflow with the full Telegram payload so it knows which job and candidate you picked.

parameters.text:
=Got it. Researching "{{ $json.topic }}".

Hang tight, pulling from 6 AI-company feeds, ranking with Claude, and drafting 3 reel scene plans. This usually takes 15-30 seconds. I'll send the candidate cards as soon as they're ready.

nodes.name: Send Acknowledgement
nodes.notes:
4. 'Got it, working on it.'

Sends an immediate Telegram reply so you know the bot is alive
and working, even though the research takes 15-30 seconds.
Set to continueOnFail, if Telegram is down, we keep going
rather than kill the whole run.

candidates.label: A
candidates.title: Scaling Codex to enterprises worldwide

beats.text: OpenAI just launched Codex Labs, and it's bringing AI-powered software development directly to the enterprise. This is the biggest push yet to get Codex running at scale inside real companies.

props.label: OPENAI CODEX
props.subhead: Enterprise-scale AI coding

beats.text: OpenAI made the announcement official, revealing that Codex is already hitting 4 million weekly active users, and that number is climbing fast.

tweet.body: Introducing Codex Labs, partnering with Accenture, PwC, Infosys & more to deploy Codex across the enterprise software development lifecycle. 4M Codex WAU and growing.

beats.text: Codex Labs is built around three pillars: deploying AI agents across the full dev lifecycle, deep integrations with enterprise toolchains, and expert partners to scale adoption fast.
The headline number: 4 million weekly active users on Codex right now. That's the usage milestone OpenAI is pointing to as proof that developer demand is real and accelerating.

props.headline: 4M Codex WAU
props.subhead: Weekly active users and growing, OpenAI's biggest coding milestone yet

beats.text: OpenAI is not doing this alone. They've locked in Accenture, PwC, Infosys, and other major systems integrators as official Codex partners, firms that already have deep roots inside Fortune 500 engineering orgs.
Inside an enterprise workflow, Codex agents can autonomously write, test, and review code, offloading entire task queues from engineering teams without a human in the loop.

props.title: ~/enterprise-repo  codex run
props.status: ✓ Task complete, 847 lines modified, 12 tests passed

logLines.text: PR ready for review, 847 lines changed

beats.text: The play spans the entire software development lifecycle, from writing and reviewing code to debugging, testing, and deployment. Codex agents are designed to plug into every stage, not just autocomplete.
OpenAI is betting that Codex Labs and its partner network will make AI coding the default inside every major enterprise. Follow to stay on top of every move.

props.label: OPENAI
props.headline: Codex is going enterprise
props.subhead: 4M WAU. Accenture. PwC. Infosys. The scale-up starts now.

candidates.label: B
candidates.title: OpenAI helps Hyatt advance AI among colleagues

beats.text: OpenAI just partnered with Hyatt to roll out ChatGPT Enterprise across its entire global workforce. This is one of the largest hospitality AI deployments ever.

props.label: OPENAI × HYATT
props.subhead: ChatGPT deployed globally across Hyatt

beats.text: The news broke straight from OpenAI's official channel, confirming Hyatt is using GPT-5.4 and Codex to transform how its teams operate.

tweet.body: Hyatt is deploying ChatGPT Enterprise across its global workforce, using GPT-5.4 and Codex to boost productivity, improve operations, and elevate guest experiences.

beats.text: Hyatt is deploying ChatGPT Enterprise for three core use cases: boosting employee productivity, streamlining operations, and enhancing guest experiences.
Hyatt operates over 1,300 properties in more than 80 countries. That's the scale at which ChatGPT Enterprise is now being put to work.

props.headline: 1,300+ Properties
props.subhead: ChatGPT Enterprise active across 80+ countries

beats.text: Hyatt's engineering teams are using Codex to automate software tasks, writing code, reviewing pull requests, and accelerating internal tooling development.

logLines.text: ✓ All checks passed, ready for review

beats.text: Enterprise AI deployments like this typically drive significant gains. Teams report faster task completion, fewer manual errors, and more time on high-value work.
The deployment runs on GPT-5.4, OpenAI's current flagship model, giving Hyatt staff access to best-in-class reasoning for everything from drafting comms to analyzing data.
OpenAI and Hyatt just set a new benchmark for enterprise AI in hospitality. Follow for daily AI updates so you never miss moves like this.

props.label: OPENAI
props.headline: Enterprise AI Goes Hospitality
props.subhead: Hyatt × ChatGPT Enterprise, 1,300+ properties, 80+ countries

candidates.label: C
candidates.title: Codex for (almost) everything

beats.text: OpenAI just gave Codex computer use, a browser, image generation, memory, and plugins, all in one desktop app. This is the biggest Codex update yet.

props.label: OPENAI CODEX
props.subhead: For macOS & Windows

beats.text: OpenAI dropped the news on X, and the developer community lit up. Here's what they posted.

tweet.body: Codex for (almost) everything, computer use, in-app browsing, image generation, memory, and plugins are now available in the Codex app for macOS and Windows.

beats.text: Five new capabilities land in a single update: computer use so Codex can control your desktop, in-app browsing to pull live data, and full image generation baked right in.
On top of that, Codex now remembers context across sessions with persistent memory, and supports plugins so you can connect your own tools and APIs.
Computer use means Codex can literally open apps, click buttons, and complete tasks on your machine, no manual handoff required.

props.title: ~/project  codex run
props.prompt: codex --task 'open browser, search docs, generate diagram'

logLines.text: Opening system browser via computer use...

beats.text: Codex is already used by millions of developers, and now with computer use plus plugins, OpenAI is betting it becomes the default AI coding environment on desktop.

props.headline: 5 Features. 1 Update.
props.subhead: Computer use, browsing, images, memory & plugins ship together for macOS and Windows

beats.text: Inside the editor, memory means Codex recalls your project conventions, preferred libraries, and past decisions, so you stop repeating yourself every session.

lines.text: // Memory: project uses Express + Zod + Prisma
// Codex recalled: always validate before DB write

beats.text: OpenAI's Codex just became a full developer agent on your desktop. Follow for daily breakdowns of every major AI update.

props.label: OPENAI
props.headline: Codex is your new dev agent
props.subhead: Computer use · Browser · Images · Memory · Plugins

candidates.label: A
candidates.title: Introducing Claude Design by Anthropic Labs - Anthropic

beats.text: Anthropic just dropped Claude Design, a brand new AI product built specifically for designers and creative work. This is straight out of Anthropic Labs, their experimental product division.

props.label: ANTHROPIC CLAUDE DESIGN
props.subhead: By Anthropic Labs

beats.text: The announcement went live directly from Anthropic, signaling this is a serious push into the creative and design workflow space.

tweet.body: Introducing Claude Design by Anthropic Labs, a new AI experience built for design and creative work. Available now.

beats.text: Claude Design is built around three core ideas: generating production-ready UI, iterating on visual concepts in real time, and understanding design intent from natural language.
Anthropic Labs is the experimental arm of a company valued at over 61 billion dollars, and Claude Design is their first dedicated creative tool shipped from that division.

props.headline: $61B Company
props.subhead: Claude Design is Anthropic Labs' first dedicated creative product.

beats.text: Imagine describing a landing page in plain English and watching Claude Design generate the full layout, component hierarchy, and color tokens, all in one pass.

props.title: ~/claude-design  generate
props.prompt: claude design --prompt "SaaS landing page, dark mode, CTA above fold"

beats.text: Where general-purpose AI tools give you raw code or generic mockups, Claude Design is trained to understand visual hierarchy, spacing systems, and brand consistency from the ground up.
One standout capability: Claude Design can iterate on your existing designs by reading screenshots or uploaded files and suggesting precise, contextual improvements.
That means less time in revision loops and more time shipping. Claude Design handles the back-and-forth so your team doesn't have to.
Claude Design is live now from Anthropic Labs. If you're a designer or product team using AI tools, this one is worth watching closely. Follow for daily AI updates.

props.label: ANTHROPIC
props.headline: Claude Design is live, try it now
props.subhead: Anthropic Labs' first creative AI product

candidates.label: B
candidates.title: How Anthropic’s Claude Design Has Already Saved Me 10 Hours of Work - inc.com

beats.text: Anthropic's Claude just saved one writer 10 full hours of work, and it's not because of raw speed. It's because of how Claude is designed to think.

props.label: ANTHROPIC CLAUDE
props.subhead: 10 Hours Saved. Here's How.

beats.text: According to a real-world test published on Inc.com, Claude's design philosophy, not just its raw output, eliminated 10 hours of manual work in a single workflow.

props.headline: 10 Hours Saved
props.subhead: Real-world workflow test, reported by Inc.com

beats.text: Claude stands out in three specific ways: it follows nuanced instructions, it asks clarifying questions instead of guessing, and it maintains context across long tasks.
Instead of generating a wall of generic text, Claude broke down a complex research brief into structured sections, then refined each one after a single follow-up prompt.

props.title: ~/workspace  claude-workflow
props.status: ✓ Task complete, 6 sections refined in 2 prompts

logLines.text: Final draft ready, estimated time saved: 4h

beats.text: Anthropic built Claude around a principle called Constitutional AI, meaning the model is trained to be helpful, harmless, and honest by design, not just by prompt engineering.
The 10 hours of savings broke down across three task types: research compilation saved 4 hours, drafting saved 3.5 hours, and editing rounds saved 2.5 hours.
Compared to other AI assistants, Claude's edge isn't benchmark scores, it's fewer correction loops. Users report finishing tasks in 2 prompts versus 5 or more with alternatives.
If your workflow involves research, writing, or editing, Claude's design could give you back hours every week. Follow for daily breakdowns of what AI tools actually do.

props.label: ANTHROPIC
props.headline: Get Your Hours Back
props.subhead: Claude's design does the heavy lifting

props.label: OPENAI CHATGPT ENTERPRISE

beats.text: The announcement dropped straight from OpenAI, confirming Hyatt as a flagship ChatGPT Enterprise customer using the latest models in production.

tweet.body: Hyatt is deploying ChatGPT Enterprise across its global workforce, using GPT-5.4 and Codex to transform productivity, operations, and guest experiences.

beats.text: The deployment runs on three pillars: GPT-5.4 for advanced reasoning, Codex for internal software development, and enterprise-grade security for a workforce spanning dozens of countries.
Hyatt operates over 1,300 properties across more than 70 countries. That's the scale ChatGPT Enterprise now has to serve, every day, across every department.

props.headline: 1,300+ Properties
props.subhead: ChatGPT Enterprise active across 70+ countries

beats.text: Codex is doing real engineering work inside Hyatt, automating repetitive code tasks, helping non-technical teams build internal tools, and accelerating software delivery.
The results target three measurable areas: employee productivity, operational efficiency, and guest experience quality, all tracked across Hyatt's global hotel network.

candidates.label: A
candidates.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech

beats.text: Google DeepMind just dropped Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS, and it changes how AI voices sound forever. This is the next generation of expressive AI speech.

props.label: GOOGLE DEEPMIND GEMINI
props.subhead: Next-Gen Expressive AI Speech

beats.text: The announcement landed on the DeepMind blog and the AI community is paying attention. Granular audio tags, precise control over how AI speaks.

tweet.body: Introducing Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS, our newest audio model with granular audio tags that give you precise control to direct AI speech for expressive audio generation.

beats.text: Three things make this model stand out: granular audio tags for scene-by-scene control, expressive tone direction, and real-time speech generation at Flash speed.
The key unlock is precision, audio tags let developers control pacing, emotion, and style at the individual sentence level. That's a first for a production TTS model at this scale.

props.headline: Sentence-Level Audio Control
props.subhead: Direct AI speech beat by beat with granular audio tags, no more one-size-fits-all voice output.

beats.text: Here's what using audio tags actually looks like in the API. You wrap your text with descriptive tags, whisper, excited, slow, and the model interprets them as real speech directions.

logLines.text: [excited] Welcome to the future of AI speech!
[whisper] This part should feel intimate and close.
Audio tag pipeline complete. 3 segments processed.

beats.text: Compare this to legacy TTS systems. Old models give you one flat voice, same tone, start to finish. Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS gives you a director's toolkit for every line.
The use cases are massive: audiobooks with character voices, dynamic ad reads, interactive game narration, and accessibility tools that actually sound human.
Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS is available now via the Google DeepMind API. If you want to stay ahead of every AI model drop, follow for daily updates.

props.label: GEMINI
props.headline: AI Speech Just Leveled Up
props.subhead: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS, granular audio tags, expressive output, available now.

props.label: GOOGLE CHROME AI MODE
props.subhead: A new way to explore the web

beats.text: The announcement dropped on the Google blog and the web community immediately took notice.

tweet.body: Today's upgrades for AI Mode in Chrome transform how you interact with the web. Ask questions, get answers, and explore, right inside your browser.

beats.text: AI Mode in Chrome ships with three headline capabilities: asking questions about any page, getting AI-generated answers inline, and exploring the web through natural conversation.
Chrome has over 3 billion active users worldwide. Putting AI Mode directly in the browser puts this tech in front of more people than any standalone AI app on the market.

props.headline: 3B+ Chrome Users
props.subhead: AI Mode now ships inside the world's most-used browser

beats.text: Here's what it looks like in practice. You open a complex article, type a question in the AI sidebar, and Chrome surfaces a grounded answer, with citations from the page itself.

props.title: AI Mode, Chrome Browser
props.subhead: Ask questions about any page. Get answers grounded in real web content.

beats.text: The AI doesn't just summarize, it reasons across multiple tabs, compares products, and helps you make decisions without leaving the browser window.

props.title: ~/chrome  ai-mode --session
props.prompt: user@chrome:~$ ask "Compare these two products across open tabs"
props.status: AI Mode: Reasoning across 3 tabs… Done ✓

logLines.text: Reading Tab 1: Product A specs page
Reading Tab 2: Product B specs page
Answer grounded in 3 sources. Citations attached.

beats.text: Google is rolling AI Mode out in phases. Search integration scores highest for maturity, followed by page summarization, and multi-tab reasoning is still expanding.
The biggest unlock is conversational browsing. No more keyword searches, just ask Chrome what you want, the way you'd ask a person.
AI Mode is live and rolling out now. Google says more features are coming, including deeper Workspace integration, in the weeks ahead.
Google just put an AI co-pilot inside 3 billion browsers. If you want to stay on top of every move in AI, follow for daily updates.

candidates.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech

beats.text: Google DeepMind just dropped Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS, their most expressive AI voice model ever. This changes how AI speaks.

props.label: GEMINI
props.subhead: Next-gen expressive AI speech

beats.text: DeepMind published the full announcement on their blog, and the AI community is buzzing.

props.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: Next-Gen Expressive AI Speech

beats.text: The model ships with three headline breakthroughs: granular audio tags, precise directorial control, and natural expressive generation.
The killer feature? Audio tags let developers specify exactly how a phrase should sound, whispering, excited, cautious, at a granular level no other model offers.

props.headline: Granular Audio Tags
props.subhead: Direct every syllable of AI speech

beats.text: Developers are already reacting, the ability to direct AI voice like a film director is being called a paradigm shift for audio apps.

tweet.body: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS audio tags are insane. I can tell the model to sound nervous, then confident, mid-sentence. This is a director's console for AI voice. 🎙️🔥

beats.text: Step one: tag your script with emotions and delivery cues.
Step two: pass it to Flash TTS and the model voices every nuance exactly as directed.
The result is AI-generated speech that finally sounds human, precise, directed, and emotionally nuanced. This is the new benchmark.

props.headline: Human-Level Expressiveness
props.subhead: Powered by granular audio control

beats.text: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS is live now. Follow for daily AI updates so you never miss the next big launch.

props.label: GEMINI 3.1 FLASH TTS
props.headline: AI speech just got expressive
props.subhead: Granular audio tags · Directorial control · Live now

beats.text: What if an AI could compress decades of drug discovery into months? That question just got a real answer.

props.headline: What if AI could compress decades of drug discovery into months?

beats.text: OpenAI just launched GPT-Rosalind, a frontier reasoning model purpose-built for life sciences research.
It targets four high-stakes domains: drug discovery, genomics analysis, protein reasoning, and scientific research workflows.
Researchers are already calling it transformative. Real scientists, real results, here's what they're saying.

testimonials.quote: GPT-Rosalind compressed months of genomic analysis work into a single afternoon. I've never seen anything like it.
testimonials.attribution: Computational biologist, Stanford Drug Discovery Lab

testimonials.quote: It doesn't just retrieve, it reasons across protein structures in ways that genuinely surprise us.
This is the before-and-after moment for our entire field. We're already restructuring our research pipeline around it.

beats.text: The announcement went viral fast. Scientists and founders flooded X with reactions.

tweet.body: GPT-Rosalind is not a chatbot for bio. It's a frontier reasoning engine. The protein inference benchmarks alone are jaw-dropping. This changes how we do science. Full stop.

beats.text: Before GPT-Rosalind, a single protein structure reasoning task could take a team weeks. Now it takes minutes.
The name honors Rosalind Franklin, the scientist whose X-ray crystallography unlocked DNA's double helix, OpenAI is betting this model carries that same legacy forward.

props.title: Named for Rosalind Franklin, Pioneer of Molecular Discovery

beats.text: GPT-Rosalind could be the model that changes how we find cures. Follow for daily AI updates.

props.label: openai
props.headline: GPT-Rosalind is rewriting life sciences research

candidates.label: C
candidates.title: The next evolution of the Agents SDK

beats.text: OpenAI just dropped the next evolution of its Agents SDK, and this update completely changes how developers build long-running AI agents.

props.label: AGENTS SDK
props.subhead: Native sandbox execution · Model-native harness

beats.text: The SDK now scores top marks on three critical developer needs: secure isolation, multi-tool orchestration, and persistent file access, all native, no third-party patches.
Agents now run inside a native sandbox, meaning code executes in an isolated environment automatically, with zero manual container setup.

logLines.text: Container isolated, no manual config required

beats.text: Before this update, developers had to wire up custom runtimes and security layers manually. Now the SDK handles all of that out of the box.

comparison.left: Custom runtimes + manual security layers + brittle glue code
comparison.right: Native sandbox + model-native harness + built-in orchestration

beats.text: The model-native harness means the agent loop is tightly coupled to the model itself, so tool calls, retries, and state are handled with fewer than 10 lines of code.
The numbers speak for themselves: agents can now span multiple files and tools in a single run, with long-running tasks staying alive across sessions.

props.headline: What's New in Agents SDK v2

beats.text: Security is first-class, every agent execution is sandboxed by default, so rogue code or unexpected tool outputs can't escape the environment.

props.headline: Secure by Default
props.subhead: Every agent run is sandboxed, no extra config needed

beats.text: OpenAI's Agents SDK is moving fast. Follow for daily breakdowns of the tools reshaping AI development.

props.label: OPENAI AGENTS SDK
props.headline: Build Smarter Agents, Today
props.subhead: Native sandboxing · Model-native harness · Zero boilerplate
